title: Beijing Axis
subtitle: Tiananmen / Palace Museum / Jingshan
blurb: Walk from Tiananmen through the Palace Museum to Jingshan, and save energy for the climb to the view.
mood: Early start, entry checks, and a long palace walk

microcopy.line: Before you leave, check the square slot, Palace Museum ticket, and passport.

content.intro:
Start early at Tiananmen and move north: the square, Meridian Gate, the main Palace Museum line, the garden, and Jingshan Hill. Inside the palace, do not try to cover every side court. Keep to the central line and add detours only if you still have energy.

The hard part here is access and control points. Keep your passport separate from your bag, check the Palace Museum booking in advance, and avoid tight plans after Jingshan: checks and crowds can take an extra hour.
content.routeLogic:
At the square, watch where the flow moves north.
At Meridian Gate, check documents before joining a queue.
Inside the Palace Museum, follow the central line and take side courts only if you have energy.
In the garden, move toward the north exit without extra loops.
Climb Jingshan if you still have 45-60 minutes and visibility is not poor.
content.bestFor:
a first day with bookings already arranged
mornings without fixed meetings after the route
travelers who want to cross the center on foot and finish above the roofs
content.notFor:
the day after a long flight
heat, heavy rain, or dense smog
when you do not have Palace Museum booking or clear Tiananmen access
content.beforeYouGo:
For long walking days, choose April-May or September-October when possible.
In July-August, start earlier: open squares heat up quickly.
The Forbidden City is closed on Mondays except holidays; book under your real name and bring the original passport.
Check Tiananmen access in the morning before leaving the hotel.
content.timing: Start in the morning. Leave time for the square and security checks, cross the Palace Museum before late day, and keep at least 45-60 minutes for Jingshan.
content.fallbackPlan: If the square is closed for passage, start from an allowed approach to Meridian Gate. If you cannot enter the Palace Museum, do not spend the day at ticket windows: move the palace to another day, go to Jingshan, and walk around the north gates.

stopList.name: Tiananmen Square
stopList.note: Check slot and passport before leaving

content.context: Do not linger on the square for every detail. First find the entrance, see how the flow moves north, and judge the security queue. A quick move toward the Palace Museum can fall apart here, so keep time in reserve.
content.lookFor:
Flow moving north
View toward Tiananmen Gate
Security queues
content.practical:
Keep your passport separate from your bag.
Check access rules on the morning of the visit.
In heat, do not stay on the open square longer than needed.
content.nextMove: Move to the north side and continue toward Meridian Gate without drifting into side streets.
content.sourceNote: Access rules and square reservation slots can change; check the official process before leaving.

content.context: At Meridian Gate, step aside from the flow and check your ticket, passport, and correct entrance. Joining the wrong queue can drain energy before you even reach the palace courts.
content.lookFor:
High wall before the entrance
Queues for checks
Signs for the entry route
content.practical:
The original passport is needed at entry.
Booking must be arranged in advance.
Do not count on buying a same-day ticket on site.
content.nextMove: After entry, keep to the central line toward the main halls and leave side courts for spare time only.
content.sourceNote: Palace Museum booking rules need a current check before the visit.

stopList.name: Forbidden City
stopList.note: Follow the main line and save energy

content.context: Inside the Forbidden City, move from gates to halls along the main line and look back often: the repeating courts read best while you are walking. Choose side areas only while you still have time and legs.
content.lookFor:
Sequence of gates
Raised hall platforms
Roof details and ridge beasts
content.practical:
The museum is closed on Mondays except holidays.
Shorten the route if it is hot or crowds are dense.
Do not try to enter every side courtyard.
content.nextMove: Move toward the north of the complex and exit through the garden so you do not backtrack.

stopList.name: Imperial Garden
stopList.note: A north-exit passage without extra loops

content.context: After the large courts, the garden brings rocks, old trees, and narrow passages. If it is crowded, do not stop at the most famous stones. Move to the edges, look at the pavilions, and keep your direction toward the north exit.
content.lookFor:
Artificial rocks
Old cypress trees
Pavilions among narrow paths
content.practical:
In crowds, avoid stopping at the most popular rocks.
If you are tired, head to the north exit without side loops.
Take photos at the edges, not in the middle of the flow.
content.nextMove: Exit through the north gate and cross into Jingshan Park.

stopList.name: Jingshan Park
stopList.note: Climb only if you have energy and visibility

content.context: Jingshan stands just behind the Palace Museum north gates. The climb is short, but after the palace it feels heavier than it looks on the map. Go slowly and pause on platforms if your legs are already tired.
content.lookFor:
View over the Forbidden City roofs
The straight axis to the south
The pavilion at the top
content.practical:
Sunset brings more people.
Steps can be slippery after rain.
If visibility is poor, climb only if you still want a short final push.
content.nextMove: After descending, take the metro or a taxi; walking to another district would be an extra haul.
